segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translation.text: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: What in particular does a person gain when he or she becomes a serious collector?

translation.text: 一个人成为真正投入其中的收藏家后，尤其能获得什么？

segments.text: People tend to amass possessions, sometimes without being aware of doing so.

translation.text: 人们往往会积聚物品，有时自己都没有意识到。

segments.text: Indeed, they can have a delightful surprise when they find something useful which they did not know they owned.

translation.text: 事实上，当他们找到一件自己都不知道拥有的有用之物时，还会感到意外的惊喜。

segments.text: Those who never have to move house become indiscriminate collectors of what can only be described as clutter.

translation.text: 那些从不需要搬家的人，会不加选择地收集各种只能称作杂物的东西。

segments.text: They leave unwanted objects in drawers, cupboards and attics for years in the belief that they may one day need just those very things.

translation.text: 他们把不用的物品放在抽屉、橱柜和阁楼里多年，认为也许有一天恰好会用到。

segments.text: As they grow old, people also accumulate belongings for two other reasons.

translation.text: 随着年龄增长，人们还会出于另外两个原因积攒物品。

segments.text: Lack of physical and mental energy, both of which are essential in turning out and throwing away, and sentiment.

translation.text: 一是缺乏清理和丢弃所必需的体力和精力，二是感情因素。

segments.text: Things owned for a long time are full of associations with the past, perhaps with relatives who are dead, and so they gradually acquire a value beyond their true worth.

translation.text: 长期拥有的物品承载着许多对往事的联想，也许还与已经去世的亲人有关，因此逐渐获得了超出实际价值的意义。

segments.text: Some things are collected deliberately in the home in an attempt to avoid waste.

translation.text: 为了避免浪费，有些东西会被人有意地收存在家里。

segments.text: Among these I would list string and brown paper, kept by thrifty people when a parcel has been opened, to save buying these two requisites.

translation.text: 其中我会列出绳子和牛皮纸；节俭的人拆开包裹后把它们留下，以免再花钱购买这两样必需品。

segments.text: Collecting small items can easily become a mania.

translation.text: 收集小物件很容易变成一种癖好。

segments.text: I know someone who always cuts sketches out from newspapers of model clothes that she would like to buy if she had the money.

translation.text: 我认识一个人，她总从报纸上剪下时装图样，那些衣服是她有钱时想买的。

segments.text: As she is not rich, the chances that she will ever be able to afford such purchases are remote.

translation.text: 由于她并不富有，将来买得起这些东西的可能性非常渺茫。

segments.text: But she is never sufficiently strong-minded to be able to stop the practice.

translation.text: 可她总是缺乏足够的意志力，无法停止这种做法。

segments.text: It is a harmless habit, but it litters up her desk to such an extent that every time she opens it, loose bits of paper fall out in every direction.

translation.text: 这习惯并无害处，却把她的书桌弄得乱七八糟，以至每次打开书桌，散落的纸片都会向四处掉出来。

segments.text: Collecting as a serious hobby is quite different and has many advantages.

translation.text: 把收藏当作严肃的爱好则完全不同，而且有许多好处。

segments.text: It provides relaxation for leisure hours, as just looking at one's treasures is always a joy.

translation.text: 它能使人闲暇时放松，因为单是欣赏自己的珍藏便始终是一种乐趣。

segments.text: One does not have to go outside for amusement since the collection is housed at home.

translation.text: 收藏品就放在家里，所以不必出门寻找消遣。

segments.text: Whatever it consists of, stamps, records, first editions of books, china, glass, antique furniture, pictures, model cars, stuffed birds, toy animals, there is always something

translation.text: 无论收藏的是邮票、唱片、初版本书籍、瓷器、玻璃器皿、古董家具、图画、汽车模型、鸟类标本还是玩具动物，总有一些

segments.text: to do in connection with it, from finding the right place for the latest edition to verifying facts in reference books.

translation.text: 与收藏有关的事情可做，从为最新藏品找到合适的位置，到在参考书中核实资料。

segments.text: This hobby educates one not only in the chosen subject, but also in general matters which have some bearing on it.

translation.text: 这种爱好不仅让人了解所选领域，也让人了解与之相关的一般知识。

segments.text: There are also other benefits.

translation.text: 它还有其他益处。

segments.text: One wants to meet like-minded collectors, to get advice, to compare notes, to exchange articles, to show off the latest find.

translation.text: 收藏者希望结识志趣相投的人，听取建议、交流心得、交换藏品并炫耀最新发现。

segments.text: So one's circle of friends grows.

translation.text: 因此，朋友圈会不断扩大。

segments.text: Soon the hobby leads to travel, perhaps to a meeting in another town.

translation.text: 很快，这项爱好还会让人踏上旅途，也许是去另一个城镇参加聚会。

segments.text: Possibly a trip abroad in search of a rare specimen, for collectors are not confined to any one country.

translation.text: 也可能为了寻找稀有藏品而出国，因为收藏家并不限于某一个国家。

segments.text: Over the years, one may well become an authority on one's hobby and will very probably be asked to give informal talks to little gatherings and then, if successful, to larger audiences.

translation.text: 多年以后，一个人很可能成为自己爱好领域的权威，并会受邀先在小型聚会上作非正式讲演；如果讲得成功，还会面对更多听众。

segments.text: In this way, self-confidence grows, first from mastering a subject, then from being able to talk about it.

translation.text: 这样，自信心便会增长：先是因为掌握了一门学问，继而是因为能够谈论它。

segments.text: Collecting, by occupying spare time so constructively, makes a person contented with no time for boredom.

translation.text: 收藏以如此有益的方式占用闲暇，使人心满意足，根本没有时间感到无聊。
